perturbation.original_text: JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each party hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each signatory hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'JOINT FILING AGREEMENT' to 'JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT' and 'party hereto' to 'signatory hereto' creates ambiguity. While the document proceeds as if this is a 'filing agreement,' the heading suggests it could be any kind of 'submission contract.' This is contradictory because the rest of the agreement repeatedly refers to the agreement as a 'filing agreement,' creating confusion over the document's true nature and the roles of the participants. The term 'party hereto' is a well-defined legal term, changing this to 'signatory hereto' might cause doubt on its meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each signatory hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto. This agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto, being duly authorized, has caused this agreement to be executed and effective as of the date set forth below. Date: April 7, 2020 BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION By: Ally Pecarro Name: Ally Pecarro Title: Attorney-in-fact BANC OF AMERICA PREFERRED FUNDING CORPORATION By: Michael Jentis Name: Michael Jentis Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'JOINT FILING AGREEMENT' to 'JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT' and 'party hereto' to 'signatory hereto'. While the agreement continues to refer to itself as a 'filing agreement,' the contradictory text in the contradicted text names it a 'JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT'. Furthermore, the contradicted text uses both 'party hereto' and 'signatory hereto'. This creates a contradiction because the true nature of the document and the roles of the involved parties become unclear. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably ('JOINT FILING AGREEMENT' and 'JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT', 'party hereto' and 'signatory hereto') without defining them, leading to conflicting interpretations.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This contract shall remain in full force and effect until terminated by any participant in a written notice provided to the other participants, and then only with respect to such terminating participant.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'agreement' is changed to 'contract,' 'revoked' is changed to 'terminated,' 'party hereto' is changed to 'participant,' and 'signed writing' is changed to 'written notice.' This introduces ambiguity regarding the formal requirements for ending the relationship. It's unclear if 'termination' carries the same legal weight as 'revocation,' especially given the document's initial labeling as an 'agreement'. Also a 'participant' doesn't have the same legal meaning as a 'party hereto'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each signatory hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto. This contract shall remain in full force and effect until terminated by any participant in a written notice provided to the other participants, and then only with respect to such terminating participant. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto, being duly authorized, has caused this agreement to be executed and effective as of the date set forth below. Date: April 7, 2020 BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION By: Ally Pecarro Name: Ally Pecarro Title: Attorney-in-fact BANC OF AMERICA PREFERRED FUNDING CORPORATION By: Michael Jentis Name: Michael Jentis Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces established legal terminology ('agreement,' 'revoked,' 'party hereto,' 'signed writing') with potentially less precise terms ('contract,' 'terminated,' 'participant,' 'written notice'). This introduces ambiguity, as the contradicted text relies on the specific legal weight of the original terms. The contradiction arises because the original text establishes a formal legal framework using defined terminology, while the changed text uses looser language, creating uncertainty about the enforceability and interpretation of the termination clause. Specifically, using 'participant' instead of 'party hereto' weakens the binding nature of the agreement, and 'written notice' instead of 'signed writing' introduces ambiguity about the level of formality required for termination.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto, being duly authorized, has caused this agreement to be executed and effective as of the date set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: AFFIRMING AGREEMENT, each member, being duly authorized, has initiated this contract to be valid and operational as of the date specified below.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "IN WITNESS WHEREOF" is changed to "AFFIRMING AGREEMENT", "party hereto" is changed to "member", "agreement" to "contract", and "executed and effective" is changed to "initiated this contract to be valid and operational." This change introduces uncertainty regarding the document's legal standing. While 'IN WITNESS WHEREOF' traditionally signifies a formal legal agreement, 'AFFIRMING AGREEMENT' doesn't hold the same established legal meaning, and the shift from 'party' to 'member' could be interpreted as changing the nature of the parties' roles, and by changing 'agreement' to 'contract', and 'executed and effective' is changed to 'initiated this contract to be valid and operational.' this is contradictory with what has been said previously in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT SUBMISSION CONTRACT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each signatory hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto. This contract shall remain in full force and effect until terminated by any participant in a written notice provided to the other participants, and then only with respect to such terminating participant. AFFIRMING AGREEMENT, each member, being duly authorized, has initiated this contract to be valid and operational as of the date specified below. Date: April 7, 2020 BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION By: Ally Pecarro Name: Ally Pecarro Title: Attorney-in-fact BANC OF AMERICA PREFERRED FUNDING CORPORATION By: Michael Jentis Name: Michael Jentis Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The original text uses formal legal language ('IN WITNESS WHEREOF,' 'party hereto,' 'agreement,' 'executed and effective') which aligns with the contradicted text's reference to the 'Securities Exchange Act of 1934' and its use of established legal terms. The changed text replaces this formal language with less legally precise terms ('AFFIRMING AGREEMENT,' 'member,' 'contract,' 'initiated this contract to be valid and operational'). This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the document's legal standing. The phrase 'AFFIRMING AGREEMENT' lacks the established legal weight of 'IN WITNESS WHEREOF,' and the shift from 'party' to 'member' could alter the understanding of the parties' roles. This misalignment in terminology and formality creates a direct conflict in how the agreement should be interpreted, hence a strong in-text contradiction.
